$comment: Studio PolicyObjects that DO NOT project to $wosWorkflow body (authoringOnly | requiresSpecExtension | unmappedButApproved). Workspace-internal authority and uncertainty tracking.

body.$comment: Cross-document supersession per source-vault.md SA-MUST-source-006/007: the federal corrective-action letter supersedes office memo §4(a). Reviewer-driven (not auto-detected).
body.supersessionRationale: Federal sub-regulatory letter (FNS Director's authority per 7 CFR §271) supersedes any conflicting state or local guidance. Office memo restricted appeal-request to written-only; federal regulation requires phone, in-person, AND written equivalently.

policyObjects.$comment_mapping: Supersession is workspace metadata; never appears in $wosWorkflow body. The CONSEQUENCE (the appeal-right policy object now allows phone+in-person+written) does project to WOS via pol-appeal-right-fair-hearing's mapping.

provenance.rationale: I read the FNS letter and verified that it supersedes office-memo §4(a). The supersession is reviewer-driven per SA-MUST-source-007.

extensionRecord.proposedSemantics: Workflow-level applicability metadata (jurisdictions, programs) at the $wosWorkflow root, complementing per-element appliesWhen FEL. Today, jurisdiction is implicit via wos-delivery sidecar's appliesWhen; promoting to a top-level field would let external verifiers parse the workflow's applicability without parsing FEL.
extensionRecord.proposedSchemaSketch: { jurisdictions: [{kind, code, displayName}], programs: [string], temporalScope: { intervals: [{start, end}] } }
extensionRecord.$comment: Per studio-to-wos-mapping.md slight extension proposals queue; until ratified, compiler emits derived FEL appliesWhen on relevant elements + an x-wos-studio.applicability envelope at the workflow root.

body.narrative: Between December 22 and January 5, redetermination 30-day-prior-notice deadlines effectively pause; notices for that window are issued the next business day after January 5.
body.rationale: Long-standing local practice in Travis County DHS based on operational reality: holiday closures + reduced staff + limited applicant availability make the 30-day window infeasible to meet during this period. Practice has been continuous since 2009 without federal or state objection. NOT in any regulation.

policyPermissibilityAttestation.$comment: SA-MUST-prov-013 + workspace.md SA-MUST-ws-043: high-assurance attestation required for local-practice claims. The workspace AuthorityGrant for compliance-reviewer authorizes attestOrigin:local-practice.
policyPermissibilityAttestation.rationale: Reviewed under Office Procedure Memo §2(b) review. Practice does not violate 7 CFR §273.10(a)(2) (the 30-day notice rule) because holiday-period notices are still issued — just batched on the next business day. The continuity-of-services framework still applies; no household is harmed.

policyObjects.$comment_mapping: No WOS counterpart. The published artifact's release notes enumerate this per SA-MUST-cmp-023.
policyObjects.unmappedRationale: Operational practice not encoded in any WOS schema concept; intentionally not generalized into a WOS extension because the practice is workspace-specific (single county). MAP-LINT-004 will fire perpetual-warn per SA-MUST-map-041.

body.$comment: Note: state §8.5 narrows federal §273.10(a)(1) for seasonal-agricultural households (3 months vs 6). Not actually a conflict — narrowing is permissible. Reviewer attested with a ReviewerResolution.
body.narrative: State manual §8.5 prescribes 3-month redetermination for seasonal-agricultural households during growing season; federal §273.10(a)(1) prescribes 6-month default. State narrows.

resolution.resolutionRationale: State narrowing is permissible per federal-state SNAP cooperative federalism. State-level deadline applies for cases matching seasonal-agricultural eligibility; federal deadline applies otherwise. No conflict to resolve; both rules coexist.

rankings.rationale: FNS Director letters supersede any conflicting state/local guidance per 7 CFR §271
State manual narrows federal where permissible; cannot widen

body.name: 7 CFR Part 273 (SNAP eligibility, redetermination, fair hearings)
